heaviest_rainfall.status: Today's storm ranked first for total rainfall with 47.89mm having fallen, indicating an exceptionally wet day.  24.6mm per hour fell at points during the storm which lasted 144.8 hours.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The 2nd ranked rain rate was recorded today with a peak rate of 24.6mm per hour, highlighting an extreme precipitation event.  47.89mm of rain fell during the storm's 144.8 hours length.  A torrential downpour occurred on 02 September 2025 when the rain reached a maximum intensity of 34.3mm per hour.  This happened during a 15.0 hour storm when 21.19mm of rain fell.

longest_rain.status: Rain fell for 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025 (ranking highest from our records for the longest storm) and it produced a total of 47.89mm of rain at a maximum intensity of 24.6mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: Since records began on 11 April 2025, the highest lightning strike count in one day is 23 on 07 September 2025.

storm.status: There is a storm in the vicinity.  This is the first storm since 08 November 2025.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: A significant rainy period is on-going and is ranked 8th for length since 23 September 2024.  So far, it has lasted 9 consecutive days beginning 07 November 2025.  The longest rainy period started on 30 November 2024 and lasted for 10 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is currently no cold snap.  This year there have been 6 cold snaps, usually there are 3.7 cold snaps per year.

extreme.status: The maximum temperature difference seen is 26.7°C, observed on 29 April 2025 when the temperature fell to 0.4°C but also managed to reach 27.1°C on the same day.

heatwaves.status: There is currently no heatwaves.  This year there have been 6 heatwavess, usually there are 1.9 heatwavess per year.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was recorded on 18 July 2025.  The total number of occurrences since 1973 number is only 62.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (183°), which differs from the daily average south-westerly (229.0°) by approximately 46.0 degrees.    In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: The top recorded wind gust is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

run.status: The top recorded wind run is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: 93.7 mph is the largest wind speed recorded on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the feels like temperature (down 2.8°C) and outside temperature (down 1.7°C) and drier conditions in rainfall (down 0.2 mm).

The past week and month had observable variation in rainfall (up 0.1 mm) and cloud levels (up 275 m) when compared to today.

Today’s average cloud levels is 130.4m higher than yesterday, 24.7m lower than the past week, 274.6m lower than the past month.

Yesterday’s cloud levels showed a low of 561m and a peak of 715m, averaging 742.0m; the week and month recorded warmer averages.

summary.summary: Inside; temperature should feel pleasantly mild. Outside; mild temperatures are expected. Also, neither too dry nor too humid and mostly calm with light air movement. Chance of light rain, the sky will be mostly cloudy (or even foggy) with low clouds, and a mix of sun and clouds. Changeable conditions.

washing.phrase: The humidity is too high (95%) for your washing to dry quickly.  Any drying will take longer than usual.
